mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in retail sales signals rising household demand and near-term revenue growth, so increases in RSAFS predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the walk-forward coefficient on 3-month RSAFS growth is nonpositive for two consecutive 3-year windows or if positive RSAFS trends do not produce positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in FEDFUNDS tightens discount rates and funding costs faster than earnings adjust, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by more than 0.50 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered FEDFUNDS increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the triggered mean return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth and falling unemployment indicate expanding labor income without recession stress, supporting consumption and earnings, so the joint signal predicts positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases over 3 months and UNRATE falls over the same 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-down signal has a nonpositive average 60-day return or fails to outperform periods with only one labor condition active.

mechanism_narrative: Falling industrial production combined with rising consumer prices signals margin pressure and stagflation risk, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines over 3 months while CPIAUCSL rises over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered stagflation instances produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or if the interaction term is not more negative than either single-series effect.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp downside break in housing starts signals weakening interest-rate-sensitive demand and future construction activity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% below its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST downside breaks are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if triggered returns are not below unconditional 60-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP and retail sales both expand, broad demand momentum raises expected corporate cash flows, so the combined demand signal predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 increases from the prior quarter and RSAFS increases over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDPC1-up and RSAFS-up condition has a nonpositive mean 60-day return or does not outperform observations where either series alone is positive.

mechanism_narrative: A material deceleration in CPI lowers expected policy pressure and discount-rate risk, supporting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: 3-month CPIAUCSL inflation rate falls by more than 0.30 percentage points while FEDFUNDS does not rise in the latest month
regime_hint: disinflation during restrictive or recently tightening policy
failure_condition: Reject if triggered disinflation episodes have a median 20-day S&P 500 return at or below zero or if returns are not higher than during CPI acceleration episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals weakening labor demand and recession risk, compressing expected earnings and risk appetite, so the signal predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.30 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE spikes are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered cases or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional 5-day average.
